design_philosophy.core_loop: Select team, play regulation basketball matches across quarters with configurable match length; compete in exhibition or tournament modes
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time basketball competition with opponent AI across multiple quarters requiring tactical team management
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Coach and play as a professional basketball team, with Pat Riley (Lakers coach) as the public face and legitimacy anchor
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Traditional basketball simulator philosophy - prioritizes authentic regulation rules (four quarters, configurable time) and official team representation over arcade simplification. Emphasizes legitimacy through real coach licensing and authentic team rosters.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can configure quarter length (5, 12, or 20 minutes) to adjust match pacing and overall game duration, providing indirect difficulty scaling through time pressure

progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight selectable teams available from start (regional and international representation: USA, Japan, Brazil, China, Italy, Mexico, England, Korea)
replayability_hooks: Eight different teams with distinct rosters to master
Tournament mode for competitive campaign play
Two-player multiplayer support for versus matches
Configurable quarter lengths for varied match experiences

world_building_techniques: Pat Riley's licensing and real-world coaching authority as brand identity
Authentic team names and regional/national representation grounding the game in real basketball landscape

faction_indicators: Eight distinct teams representing different nations and regions (USA, Japan, Brazil, China, Italy, Mexico, England, Korea) allowing team loyalty and regional affiliation
environmental_storytelling: Team selection screen implicitly communicates international basketball competition scope
Tournament mode structure suggests competitive hierarchy and championship aspirations
